resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def reversePrefix(self, word: str, ch: str) -> str:
        index = word.find(ch)
        if index != -1:
            return word[:index + 1][::-1] + word[index + 1:]
        return word
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses the built-in find method to locate the index of the character ch in the string word. If the character is found, it reverses the substring from the start of the string to the index of the character and concatenates the rest of the string. If the character is not found, it returns the original string. The efficiency is high because the find method has a linear time complexity and the string slicing and concatenation operations also have a linear time complexity. The space complexity is also linear because in the worst case, we need to create a new string that is the same as the original string.
